info.name: Flood Response
info.description:
7-agent autonomous flood response coordinator system for Lakhimpur, Assam.

Base URL: http://localhost:8000

All endpoints are pre-configured. Import this collection and start testing immediately.

request.description: Returns all resources (boats, helicopters, trucks, medical teams) from the database with GPS coordinates, status, and inventory.
Returns all evacuation shelters with capacity, current occupancy, and supply stock levels (food, water, medicine).
Returns all flood alerts sorted newest first. Includes severity level, discharge Q value, FHI score, and affected revenue circles.
Returns all SOS distress events sorted newest first. Includes phone, GPS, people count, triage level, and assignment status.
Returns all audit log entries sorted newest first. Every agent decision is logged here — conflict resolutions include both scores and the explanation.

urlencoded.description: Caller phone number (Twilio sends this automatically)

request.description:
Simulates an inbound SMS from Twilio. Parses the SMS body, creates an SOS event in the DB, pushes to sos_queue, and broadcasts sos_created via WebSocket.

SMS format: "lat,lng,people_count,injury_description"
Example: "27.23,94.10,5,injured elderly person"

WebSocket endpoint for real-time dashboard updates. Connect using Postman's WebSocket tab (not this HTTP request).

URL: ws://localhost:8000/ws

Events you will receive:
- flood_alert
- resource_moved
- sos_created
- dispatch_assigned
- conflict_raised
- conflict_resolved
- shelter_updated
- sms_sent

Each event is JSON: { "event": "<type>", "data": { ... } }
